lines: +// Pin the major version so the compiled binary is reproducible and doesn't pick
+// up a breaking `@std/http` release on a later rebuild.
// `vite build` emits a static site into `dist/`. Resolve it against the VFS in
// the compiled binary via import.meta.dirname rather than the runtime CWD.
+    // The remote `@std/http` dependency must be version-pinned so the compiled
+    // A plain Vite SPA (vite.config.ts, no server.{js,ts,mjs}) has no server
+    // entrypoint, so framework detection generates a static-file-server
+    // entrypoint that imports `@std/http` and serves the built `dist/`. This
+    // guards the whole SPA path: detect Vite, run the build task, resolve the
+    // generated entrypoint's `@std/http` import, include `dist/`, bundle, and
+    // compile into a binary — then boot that binary. The `@std/http` import is
+    // remapped to a local stub in `deno.json` so the test stays hermetic. The
+    // run step sets `EXIT_ON_LOAD` so the stub (which the entrypoint imports
+    // before its blocking `Deno.serve`) prints a marker and exits, proving the
+    // compiled binary starts and resolves the remapped import from the VFS
+    // without hanging the harness on a live server.
+// `deno.json`) so the compile doesn't need network access. The generated
+// entrypoint only imports `serveDir`; the real serving behavior is exercised by
+// This module's top level runs when the generated entrypoint imports it, before
+// that entrypoint reaches its blocking `Deno.serve`. The run step sets
+// `EXIT_ON_LOAD` so the compiled binary boots — resolving this
+// import-map-remapped specifier out of the embedded VFS — prints a marker, then
+// exits cleanly instead of hanging the harness on a live server.
+// Stand in for `vite build`: emit a minimal static site into `dist/`, including
+// a hashed asset so the compiled binary bundles the whole directory.
